evidence: `PLAN_CATALOG` defines free, starter, pro, teams, business, and enterprise with quotas and seats; `/v1/plans` returns this catalog.
risk: The API has a single code catalog for plan ids, prices, quotas, and seats.
recommended_action: Export this catalog to docs/site generation to remove drift with static pages.

evidence: Paid signup provisions the tenant on free quota, records `pending_plan`, and returns a Stripe Payment Link when configured.
risk: This prevents immediate paid quota access before payment, but tenants can remain in pending state when billing links are missing.
recommended_action: Expire pending plans and surface billing-not-configured distinctly from a real checkout flow.

evidence: `node --test tests/stripe.test.js` passed 10/10 outside the sandbox after sandbox spawn EPERM.
risk: Signature, amount mapping, and checkout URL helper logic are unit-tested.
recommended_action: Keep helper tests and add route-level webhook tests with raw-body Express handling.

evidence: Webhook verifies `stripe-signature` with `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` against raw body mounted by `server.js`.
risk: The signature path is structurally correct for Stripe webhooks.
recommended_action: Add integration tests that unsigned, stale, tampered, and signed events hit `/v1/stripe/webhook` through Express.

evidence: Local route smoke posted a signed `checkout.session.completed` with `amount_total:1234` and tenant `pending_plan:'enterprise'`; webhook activated enterprise and quota 10000000.
risk: P0/P1 revenue leakage: any signed Checkout Session in the Stripe account with the tenant id can activate an expensive pending plan even when the paid amount does not match.
recommended_action: Require amount/price/product metadata to match the target plan; never fall back to `pending_plan` without Stripe-side plan metadata validation.

evidence: Checkout completion requires `client_reference_id`; `findTenantByEmail` is imported/commented for fallback but not used.
risk: If Payment Link parameters are stripped or misconfigured, paid checkout will not activate the tenant even when Stripe has the customer email.
recommended_action: Either remove the unused fallback claim or implement safe email fallback only when email is verified and pending plan matches.

evidence: Webhook inserts `stripe_events` before tenant and plan validation, then returns warnings for missing tenant or plan.
risk: A malformed or misconfigured event id can become idempotent before the underlying billing issue is fixed, preventing simple replay.
recommended_action: Record processing status and allow safe reprocessing for warning/error events.

evidence: `customer.subscription.deleted` downgrades to free and marks cancelled, but does not clear `stripe_subscription_id` or `stripe_customer_id`.
risk: Account state can show stale Stripe identifiers after cancellation.
recommended_action: Null the deleted subscription id and preserve customer id only if needed for history.

evidence: `invoice.payment_failed` and `customer.subscription.updated` mark `billing_status:'past_due'`, but auth/quota middleware does not restrict past-due tenants.
risk: Past-due accounts can continue using paid quota until quota is exhausted or subscription is deleted.
recommended_action: Define grace period and enforcement behavior for past_due, unpaid, cancelled, and disputed states.

evidence: `authMiddleware` blocks when `t.used >= t.quota` and returns HTTP 429 `monthly quota exceeded`.
risk: Quota exists, but public API docs advertise 402 and some routes do not charge usage.
recommended_action: Align docs/status code, add route-level quota tests, and decide whether 402 or 429 is canonical.

evidence: Local route smoke completed `/v1/compile?sync=1` with `usedAfter:0`; the compile route has no `chargeUsage` call.
risk: P1 revenue/model gap: the pricing strategy says the paid unit is compile/artifact governance, but the current core compile endpoint is free after auth.
recommended_action: Charge compile jobs on accepted/completed artifacts or introduce compile-credit accounting separate from runtime quota.

evidence: `chargeUsage` is called in synthesize, synthesize/stream, synthesize/batch, publish, run, compose, label-corpus, label-corpus/stream, and specialist run.
risk: Usage accounting covers several compute paths but not compile, verify, recall, embed, capture, public run, or specialist training.
recommended_action: Create a route-to-billing matrix and intentionally mark each route as billable, free, admin, or public capped.

evidence: `POST /v1/verify` compiles and runs user-supplied source under `publishLimiter` but does not charge usage.
risk: Verifier CPU/sandbox work can be consumed without quota accounting.
recommended_action: Charge verifier work or make it explicitly free but tightly rate-limited and trust-scoped.

evidence: `/v1/recall`, `/v1/embed`, and capture proxy routes do not call `chargeUsage`.
risk: Recall ingestion and capture can become high-volume storage/compute costs outside plan limits.
recommended_action: Add storage, ingest, and capture units or explicit preview caps.

evidence: `chargeUsage` writes `used: (tenant_record.used || 0) + units`, using the request's stale tenant object rather than an atomic increment against current storage.
risk: Concurrent requests can lose increments or overshoot quota; large post-auth charges can pass the preflight quota check then push usage beyond quota.
recommended_action: Implement atomic increments or transactional billing rows and reserve quota before expensive work.

evidence: Plans store quotas and seats, but no code-level gates were found for private artifact count, seats, SSO, SCIM, project scopes, audit log, support, BAA, or enterprise controls.
risk: Pricing page features can be sold without implementation or enforcement, creating support and trust risk.
recommended_action: Separate shipped gates from roadmap promises and wire feature flags/entitlements before public paid rollout.

evidence: Code immediately downgrades canceling tenants to free quota; account UI and API docs say quotas drop at period end.
risk: Customers may lose quota immediately despite UI/API copy promising period-end access.
recommended_action: Decide immediate versus period-end cancellation and make route, Stripe, docs, and UI match.

evidence: Account UI says delete removes all artifacts and tenants permanently; code only soft-deletes the tenant row.
risk: Deletion copy overpromises data removal and conflicts with the data-lifecycle smoke.
recommended_action: Update UI/API copy or implement actual purge workflow.

evidence: `public/api.html` examples show signup/account Pro quota 100000 and seats 3, while code uses Pro quota 200000 and seats 1; docs show Business seats 15, code uses 25.
risk: Developers will integrate against stale response shapes and limits.
recommended_action: Generate API examples from live fixtures or add docs tests for plan catalog values.

evidence: `public/api.html` advertises HTTP 402 for quota exhaustion; auth middleware returns 429.
risk: SDKs and clients may handle the wrong status code for quota exhaustion.
recommended_action: Pick one canonical status and update code/tests/docs together.

evidence: Payment Links are configured by env vars, and the webhook trusts session amount or pending plan, but no Stripe Price/Product id or metadata is checked.
risk: Misconfigured links or alternate links in the same Stripe account can map to the wrong plan.
recommended_action: Include expected Stripe price ids in config and validate session line items or metadata before plan activation.

evidence: Existing tests cover Stripe helper functions only; no tests exercise `/v1/signup` paid pending flow, `/v1/account/change-plan`, `/v1/stripe/webhook`, cancel, delete, quota status, or route charging.
risk: Billing regressions can ship while helper tests remain green.
recommended_action: Add `billing-routes.test.js` with raw-body webhook, payment success/failure, pending-plan mismatch denial, route charging, and docs-shape snapshots.
